rationale: Ζ-A0..Ζ-A2 now freezes bounded staged validation, proof-only distributed replay verification, and proof-only bounded cutover rehearsal as explicit first-wave doctrine.
The completed first-wave packet is strong enough to preserve the perimeter and stop later work from collapsing doctrine into realization.
The completed first-wave prompts were doctrine-and-registry prompts, not realization prompts, so they did not materially narrow the central later-wave blocker stack.
Trust-root posture remains empty, trust and replication registries remain provisional, and provenance vocabulary remains distinct from a realized live receipt pipeline.
The next honest move is a later-wave admission and gate-refresh band that consumes the completed first-wave packet before any later-wave family is admitted.
Skipping that narrow planning band would widen scope from naming convenience instead of blocker reduction.

Broader Ζ remains blocked by replication proof, distributed replay and proof-anchor continuity realization, runtime cutover proof, distributed trust convergence, live trust/publication execution realization, and live receipt-pipeline realization.
Relocation and other perimeter live-ops families remain future-only.
The completed first-wave doctrines clarify what later-wave work must preserve, but they do not yet narrow the authority-transfer, trust-convergence, or receipt-pipeline blockers materially.
Runtime, trust, and provenance substrate remains precursor evidence rather than gate-passing realization.
A later-wave admission refresh is therefore required before any later-wave family can be admitted honestly.
cautions: First-wave completion does not count as later-wave blocker closure.
No first-wave family may be widened into handoff, convergence, or live execution claims by convenience.
Later-wave admission must remain subordinate to active checkpoint law rather than code-surface optimism.

first_wave_family_findings.title: Rollback-bearing staged transition validation
reason: Ζ-A0 freezes the family as bounded, staged, rollback-bearing, refusal-aware, and non-realized.
The family clarified first-wave legality but did not close cutover, transfer, trust, or receipt-pipeline blockers.
required_guardrails: keep it rollback-bearing refusal-aware staged and bounded
do not widen it into live cutover authority handoff trust execution or receipt-pipeline realization

first_wave_family_findings.title: Distributed replay and proof-anchor verification
reason: Ζ-A1 freezes the family as proof-only blocker-reduction work.
Deterministic replication, proof-anchor continuity realization, and distributed trust convergence remain open.
required_guardrails: do not reinterpret logs replay windows or proof-anchor lineage as realized distributed continuity

first_wave_family_findings.title: Bounded runtime cutover proof rehearsal
reason: Ζ-A2 freezes the family as rehearsal-only and proof-only.
Runtime cutover proof, authority-transfer legality, and live receipt-pipeline realization remain unresolved.
required_guardrails: do not widen it into live runtime swap claims state transfer trust execution or canonical receipt-pipeline realization

later_wave_family_findings.title: Trust-aware refusal and containment
reason: Trust-root posture remains empty and first-wave doctrine did not narrow live trust execution or revocation realization.

later_wave_family_findings.title: Live cutover receipt pipeline and anchorization
reason: First-wave doctrine sharpened receipt expectations but did not realize or materially narrow the live receipt pipeline blocker.
Provenance classification remains vocabulary and artifact law rather than canonical live receipt-pipeline execution.

later_wave_family_findings.title: Bounded authority handoff and state transfer
reason: Authority handoff remains central to unresolved replication, replay, cutover-proof, trust-convergence, and receipt-pipeline blockers.
First-wave doctrine explicitly refused to smuggle transfer semantics into validation, verification, or rehearsal.

later_wave_family_findings.title: Live trust, revocation, and publication execution
reason: Trust roots remain empty, trust policy posture remains provisional, and live execution machinery remains unrealized.
First-wave doctrine did not narrow those blockers.

later_wave_family_findings.title: Live shard relocation
reason: Relocation remains future-only and outside bounded near-term Ζ.
No first-wave artifact reduced the authority-transfer, replication, convergence, and receipt prerequisites that relocation depends on.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.reason: Later-wave family admission has not yet been re-reconciled against the completed first-wave doctrine packet.
Replication policy substrate exists, but proof-backed replication and lawful state-partition transfer remain unrealized.
Replay and proof-anchor verification doctrine now exists, but verification did not become realized distributed continuity.
Cutover-proof rehearsal doctrine now exists, but lawful cutover proof across the frozen boundaries remains unresolved.
Trust-aware and distributed-authority doctrine exists, but convergence realization remains absent.
Trust, revocation, and publication law remains explicit, but live execution remains outside the completed first-wave perimeter.
Generalized receipt law and cutover-sensitive evidence law exist, but canonical live receipt-pipeline realization remains future work.
Relocation remains perimeter-only and must not be collapsed into later-wave bounded entry by convenience.
Cluster-of-clusters, restartless-core-by-default, and similar shapes remain beyond bounded near-term Ζ.

must_avoid_replacing: the frozen first-wave perimeter with convenience widening
proof-only or rehearsal-only first-wave doctrine with live-ops language drift
active checkpoint law with stale blueprint numbering or legacy Ζ placeholders

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains projection-facing
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
first-wave guardrails must not be loosened by convenience runtime familiarity or packaging phrasing

reason: The repo now has explicit first-wave doctrine for staged validation, distributed replay verification, and bounded cutover rehearsal.
That doctrine clarifies what later-wave work must preserve, but it does not materially narrow the authority-transfer, trust-convergence, or receipt-pipeline blockers that later-wave entry depends on.
The honest next step is therefore a narrow later-wave admission and gate-refresh band, not direct later-wave family execution.

stability.replacement_target: later post-Ζ-B checkpoint may refine readiness judgments without replacing the checkpoint law frozen here
